children.heading: Agricultural Act of 1949
children.label: 1 Agricultural Act of 1949
children.text: Except in , the term “Agricultural Act of 1949” means the Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.), as in effect prior to the suspensions under .section 7301 of this title7 U.S.C. 1421section 7301(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Considered planted
children.label: 2 Considered planted
children.text: The term “considered planted” means acreage that is considered planted under title V of the Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.) and such other acreage as the Secretary considers fair and equitable.7 U.S.C. 1461

children.heading: Contract
children.label: 3 Contract
children.text: The terms “contract” and “production flexibility contract” mean a production flexibility contract entered into under .section 7211 of this title

children.heading: Contract acreage
children.label: 4 Contract acreage
children.text: The term “contract acreage” means 1 or more crop acreage bases established for contract commodities under title V of the Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.) that would have been in effect for the 1996 crop (but for suspension under ).7 U.S.C. 1461section 7301(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Contract commodity
children.label: 5 Contract commodity
children.text: The term “contract commodity” means wheat, corn, grain sorghum, barley, oats, upland cotton, and rice.

children.heading: Contract payment
children.label: 6 Contract payment
children.text: The term “contract payment” means a payment made under this subchapter pursuant to a contract.1So in original. Probably should be “chapter”.1

children.heading: Department
children.label: 7 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.label: A
children.text: is produced from pure strain varieties of the Barbadense species or any hybrid thereof, or other similar types of extra long staple cotton, designated by the Secretary, having characteristics needed for various end uses for which United States upland cotton is not suitable and grown in irrigated cotton-growing regions of the United States designated by the Secretary or other areas designated by the Secretary as suitable for the production of the varieties or types; and

children.label: B
children.text: is ginned on a roller-type gin or, if authorized by the Secretary, ginned on another type gin for experimental purposes.

children.heading: Farm program payment yield
children.label: 9 Farm program payment yield
children.text: The term “farm program payment yield” means the farm program payment yield established for the 1995 crop of a contract commodity under section 505 of the Agricultural Act of 1949 (). The Secretary shall adjust the farm program payment yield for the 1995 crop of a contract commodity to account for any additional yield payments made with respect to that crop under subsection (b)(2) of the section.7 U.S.C. 1465

children.heading: Loan commodity
children.label: 10 Loan commodity
children.text: The term “loan commodity” means each contract commodity, extra long staple cotton, and oilseed.

children.heading: Oilseed
children.label: 11 Oilseed
children.text: The term “oilseed” means a crop of soybeans, sunflower seed, rapeseed, canola, safflower, flaxseed, mustard seed, or, if designated by the Secretary, other oilseeds.

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 12 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means an owner, operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper who shares in the risk of producing a crop and who is entitled to share in the crop available for marketing from the farm, or would have shared had the crop been produced. In determining whether a grower of hybrid seed is a producer, the Secretary shall not take into consideration the existence of a hybrid seed contract.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 13 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 15 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.
